✓ TOOL RESULT

{"id":"657d19ed-babb-452d-9dec-55b6255b500d","identifier":"FRG-255","title":"Semantic similarity ranking for search results","description":"## What

Rank results by semantic similarity to the idea description, not just keyword overlap.

## Scope

* Embed the idea description and each result's description/title
* Rank results by cosine similarity
* Use an embedding API (OpenAI embeddings or similar)
* Results that are conceptually similar rank higher even without shared keywords

## Acceptance Criteria

* Results are reordered by semantic relevance
* A search for \"document formatter\" surfaces PDF/formatting tools even if they don't contain those exact words
* Signal score incorporates semantic similarity","projectMilestone":{"id":"690da7a4-d5de-4069-aa14-00aab172ab85","name":"M2: Semantic Search"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-255/semantic-similarity-ranking-for-search-results","gitBranchName":"devlab/frg-255-semantic-similarity-ranking-for-search-results","createdAt":"2026-03-09T05:04:07.820Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-09T10:36:08.905Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Dev in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Project Manager","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Project Manager","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Prior Art Scanner","projectId":"4c456b8e-532a-45d5-9bea-67dde700b251","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

USERMESSAGE #9
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→# priorart
     2→
     3→Prior art scanner for ideas. Searches GitHub repositories and Hacker News discussions to assess how crowded a space is, then produces a **Reality Signal** score (0–100).
     4→
     5→## Installation
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→pip install priorart
     9→```
    10→
    11→Or with `uv`:
    12→
    13→```bash
    14→uv add priorart
    15→```
    16→
    17→## Usage
    18→
    19→```bash
    20→# Basic scan (table output)
    21→priorart scan 'AI personal finance tracker'
    22→
    23→# JSON output
    24→priorart scan 'AI personal finance tracker' --format json
    25→
    26→# Markdown output (same format as GitHub Action comment)
    27→priorart scan 'AI personal finance tracker' --format markdown
    28→
    29→# Limit to specific sources
    30→priorart scan 'AI personal finance tracker' --sources hn
    31→priorart scan 'AI personal finance tracker' --sources github,hn
    32→
    33→# With explicit GitHub token
    34→priorart scan 'AI personal finance tracker' --github-token ghp_...
    35→```
    36→
    37→The `GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable is used automatically if set:
    38→
    39→```bash
    40→export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...
    41→priorart scan 'AI personal finance tracker' --format json
    42→```
    43→
    44→## Output formats
    45→
    46→### JSON (`--format json`)
    47→
    48→```json
    49→{
    50→  "query": "AI personal finance tracker",
    51→  "queries": ["AI personal finance tracker", "AI personal finance"],
    52→  "sources": [
    53→    {
    54→      "type": "github",
    55→      "results": [
    56→        {
    57→          "name": "org/repo",
    58→          "stars": 1234,
    59→          "description": "...",
    60→          "url": "https://github.com/org/repo",
    61→          "updated": "2024-01-01",
    62→          "language": "Python"
    63→        }
    64→      ]
    65→    },
    66→    {
    67→      "type": "hn",
    68→      "results": [
    69→        {
    70→          "title": "...",
    71→          "points": 42,
    72→          "comments": 17,
    73→          "url": "https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=...",
    74→          "date": "2024-03-01"
    75→        }
    76→      ]
    77→    }
    78→  ],
    79→  "signal": {
    80→    "score": 45,
    81→    "verdict": "MODERATE",
    82→    "breakdown": {
    83→      "github": 30,
    84→      "hn": 15
    85→    }
    86→  },
    87→  "meta": {
    88→    "timestamp": "2026-03-07T00:00:00+00:00",
    89→    "version": "0.1.0"
    90→  }
    91→}
    92→```
    93→
    94→### Markdown (`--format markdown`)
    95→
    96→Produces the same Markdown comment format used by the `prior-art-scan` GitHub Action.
    97→
    98→### Table (`--format table`)
    99→
   100→Plain-text table for terminal output (default).
   101→
   102→## Reality Signal
   103→
   104→The Reality Signal (0–100) reflects how crowded the solution space is:
   105→
   106→| Score | Verdict | Meaning |
   107→|-------|---------|---------|
   108→| 0–29 | 🟢 LOW | Greenfield opportunity or underserved niche |
   109→| 30–60 | 🟡 MODERATE | Some existing solutions, differentiation needed |
   110→| 61–100 | 🔴 HIGH | Crowded space, needs strong differentiator |
   111→
   112→The score is composed of:
   113→- **GitHub component (0–60):** number of repos found + max star count
   114→- **HN component (0–40):** number of discussions found + max point count
   115→
   116→## Development
   117→
   118→```bash
   119→# Install with dev dependencies
   120→pip install -e ".[dev]"
   121→
   122→# Run tests
   123→pytest
   124→
   125→# Lint
   126→ruff check src tests
   127→```
   128→
   129→## Package structure
   130→
   131→```
   132→src/priorart/
   133→├── __init__.py       # version
   134→├── scanner.py        # orchestrator + query builder
   135→├── scoring.py        # reality signal computation
   136→├── formatters.py     # JSON, markdown, table output
   137→├── cli.py            # typer CLI entry point
   138→└── sources/
   139→    ├── __init__.py
   140→    ├── github.py     # GitHub repository search
   141→    └── hn.py         # Hacker News search (Algolia API)
   142→```
   143→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

     1→"""Reality signal computation from search results."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→
     6→def compute_signal(
     7→    repos: list[dict],
     8→    hn_posts: list[dict],
     9→) -> dict:
    10→    """Compute a 0-100 reality signal from search results.
    11→
    12→    Returns a dict with score, verdict, and breakdown.
    13→    """
    14→    # GitHub component (0-60)
    15→    total_repos = len(repos)
    16→    max_stars = max((r["stars"] for r in repos), default=0)
    17→
    18→    if total_repos == 0:
    19→        gh_score = 0
    20→    elif total_repos <= 3:
    21→        gh_score = 15
    22→    elif total_repos <= 7:
    23→        gh_score = 30
    24→    else:
    25→        gh_score = 45
    26→
    27→    if max_stars >= 5000:
    28→        gh_score = min(60, gh_score + 20)
    29→    elif max_stars >= 1000:
    30→        gh_score = min(60, gh_score + 15)
    31→    elif max_stars >= 100:
    32→        gh_score = min(60, gh_score + 10)
    33→
    34→    # HN component (0-40)
    35→    total_hn = len(hn_posts)
    36→    max_points = max((p["points"] for p in hn_posts), default=0)
    37→
    38→    if total_hn == 0:
    39→        hn_score = 0
    40→    elif total_hn <= 2:
    41→        hn_score = 10
    42→    else:
    43→        hn_score = 25
    44→
    45→    if max_points >= 100:
    46→        hn_score = min(40, hn_score + 15)
    47→    elif max_points >= 30:
    48→        hn_score = min(40, hn_score + 10)
    49→
    50→    score = min(100, gh_score + hn_score)
    51→
    52→    if score < 30:
    53→        verdict = "LOW"
    54→    elif score <= 60:
    55→        verdict = "MODERATE"
    56→    else:
    57→        verdict = "HIGH"
    58→
    59→    return {
    60→        "score": score,
    61→        "verdict": verdict,
    62→        "breakdown": {
    63→            "github": gh_score,
    64→            "hn": hn_score,
    65→        },
    66→    }
    67→

GitHub component (0-60)\n    15\u2192    total_repos = len(repos)\n    16\u2192    max_stars = max((r[\"stars\"] for r in repos), default=0)\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192    if total_repos == 0:\n    19\u2192        gh_score = 0\n    20\u2192    elif total_repos <= 3:\n    21\u2192        gh_score = 15\n    22\u2192    elif total_repos <= 7:\n    23\u2192        gh_score = 30\n    24\u2192    else:\n    25\u2192        gh_score = 45\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192    if max_stars >= 5000:\n    28\u2192        gh_score = min(60, gh_score + 20)\n    29\u2192    elif max_stars >= 1000:\n    30\u2192        gh_score = min(60, gh_score + 15)\n    31\u2192    elif max_stars >= 100:\n    32\u2192        gh_score = min(60, gh_score + 10)\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192    # HN component (0-40)\n    35\u2192    total_hn = len(hn_posts)\n    36\u2192    max_points = max((p[\"points\"] for p in hn_posts), default=0)\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192    if total_hn == 0:\n    39\u2192        hn_score = 0\n    40\u2192    elif total_hn <= 2:\n    41\u2192        hn_score = 10\n    42\u2192    else:\n    43\u2192        hn_score = 25\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192    if max_points >= 100:\n    46\u2192        hn_score = min(40, hn_score + 15)\n    47\u2192    elif max_points >= 30:\n    48\u2192        hn_score = min(40, hn_score + 10)\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192    score = min(100, gh_score + hn_score)\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192    if score < 30:\n    53\u2192        verdict = \"LOW\"\n    54\u2192    elif score <= 60:\n    55\u2192        verdict = \"MODERATE\"\n    56\u2192    else:\n    57\u2192        verdict = \"HIGH\"\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192    return {\n    60\u2192        \"score\": score,\n    61\u2192        \"verdict\": verdict,\n    62\u2192        \"breakdown\": {\n    63\u2192            \"github\": gh_score,\n    64\u2192            \"hn\": hn_score,\n    65\u2192        },\n    66\u2192    }\n    67\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. USERMESSAGE #19
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→"""Orchestrator — builds queries, runs sources, computes signal."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import re
     6→import time
     7→from datetime import datetime, timezone
     8→
     9→from priorart import __version__
    10→from priorart.scoring import compute_signal
    11→from priorart.sources.github import search_repos
    12→from priorart.sources.hn import search_discussions
    13→
    14→
    15→def build_queries(title: str, body: str = "") -> list[str]:
    16→    """Build search queries from the idea title (and optional body).
    17→
    18→    Uses the title directly plus cleaned variants.
    19→    The title IS the idea description — no LLM needed.
    20→    """
    21→    clean = title.strip()
    22→    for prefix in ["idea:", "idea -", "[idea]"]:
    23→        if clean.lower().startswith(prefix):
    24→            clean = clean[len(prefix) :].strip()
    25→
    26→    queries: list[str] = []
    27→    seen: set[str] = set()
    28→
    29→    def add(q: str) -> None:
    30→        q = q.strip()
    31→        if q and q.lower() not in seen:
    32→            seen.add(q.lower())
    33→            queries.append(q)
    34→
    35→    # Split on common separators
    36→    parts = [clean]
    37→    for sep in [" — ", " - ", " – ", " | "]:
    38→        if sep in clean:
    39→            parts = [p.strip() for p in clean.split(sep) if p.strip()]
    40→            break
    41→
    42→    for part in parts:
    43→        stripped = re.sub(r"\([^)]*\)", "", part).strip()
    44→        stripped = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]", " ", stripped).strip()
    45→        stripped = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", stripped)
    46→        if stripped:
    47→            if len(stripped.split()) <= 1 and len(parts) > 1:
    48→                continue
    49→            add(stripped)
    50→
    51→    full = " ".join(queries)
    52→    words = full.split()
    53→    if len(words) > 5:
    54→        add(" ".join(words[:5]))
    55→        add(" ".join(words[:3]))
    56→
    57→    if body:
    58→        for line in body.split("
"):
    59→            line = line.strip().strip("#").strip("-").strip("*").strip()
    60→            if len(line) > 10 and not line.startswith(("http", "![", "<!--")):
    61→                body_clean = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]", " ", line).strip()
    62→                body_clean = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", body_clean)
    63→                body_words = body_clean.split()
    64→                if len(body_words) >= 3:
    65→                    add(" ".join(body_words[:6]))
    66→                    break
    67→
    68→    return queries
    69→
    70→
    71→def scan(
    72→    idea: str,
    73→    github_token: str | None = None,
    74→    sources: list[str] | None = None,
    75→    body: str = "",
    76→) -> dict:
    77→    """Run a full prior-art scan and return structured results.
    78→
    79→    Args:
    80→        idea: The idea description to scan.
    81→        github_token: GitHub personal access token (required for GitHub source).
    82→        sources: List of source names to query. Defaults to ["github", "hn"].
    83→        body: Optional body text for additional query extraction.
    84→
    85→    Returns:
    86→        Structured dict: {query, queries, sources, signal, meta}
    87→    """
    88→    if sources is None:
    89→        sources = ["github", "hn"]
    90→
    91→    queries = build_queries(idea, body)
    92→
    93→    all_repos: list[dict] = []
    94→    all_hn: list[dict] = []
    95→    seen_repo_names: set[str] = set()
    96→    seen_hn_urls: set[str] = set()
    97→
    98→    for query in queries:
    99→        if "github" in sources:
   100→            repos = search_repos(query, github_token)
   101→            for r in repos:
   102→                if r["name"] not in seen_repo_names:
   103→                    seen_repo_names.add(r["name"])
   104→                    all_repos.append(r)
   105→            time.sleep(1)
   106→
   107→        if "hn" in sources:
   108→            hn = search_discussions(query)
   109→            for h in hn:
   110→                if h["url"] not in seen_hn_urls:
   111→                    seen_hn_urls.add(h["url"])
   112→                    all_hn.append(h)
   113→            time.sleep(0.5)
   114→
   115→    all_repos.sort(key=lambda r: r["stars"], reverse=True)
   116→    all_hn.sort(key=lambda h: h["points"], reverse=True)
   117→
   118→    signal = compute_signal(all_repos, all_hn)
   119→
   120→    source_results = []
   121→    if "github" in sources:
   122→        source_results.append({"type": "github", "results": all_repos})
   123→    if "hn" in sources:
   124→        source_results.append({"type": "hn", "results": all_hn})
   125→
   126→    return {
   127→        "query": idea,
   128→        "queries": queries,
   129→        "sources": source_results,
   130→        "signal": signal,
   131→        "meta": {
   132→            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   133→            "version": __version__,
   134→        },
   135→    }
   136→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

   32\u2192            seen.add(q.lower())\n    33\u2192            queries.append(q)\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192    # Split on common separators\n    36\u2192    parts = [clean]\n    37\u2192    for sep in [\" \u2014 \", \" - \", \" \u2013 \", \" | \"]:\n    38\u2192        if sep in clean:\n    39\u2192            parts = [p.strip() for p in clean.split(sep) if p.strip()]\n    40\u2192            break\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192    for part in parts:\n    43\u2192        stripped = re.sub(r\"\\([^)]*\\)\", \"\", part).strip()\n    44\u2192        stripped = re.sub(r\"[^\\w\\s-]\", \" \", stripped).strip()\n    45\u2192        stripped = re.sub(r\"\\s+\", \" \", stripped)\n    46\u2192        if stripped:\n    47\u2192            if len(stripped.split()) <= 1 and len(parts) > 1:\n    48\u2192                continue\n    49\u2192            add(stripped)\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    full = \" \".join(queries)\n    52\u2192    words = full.split()\n    53\u2192    if len(words) > 5:\n    54\u2192        add(\" \".join(words[:5]))\n    55\u2192        add(\" \".join(words[:3]))\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192    if body:\n    58\u2192        for line in body.split(\"\\n\"):\n    59\u2192            line = line.strip().strip(\"#\").strip(\"-\").strip(\"*\").strip()\n    60\u2192            if len(line) > 10 and not line.startswith((\"http\", \"![\", \"<!--\")):\n    61\u2192                body_clean = re.sub(r\"[^\\w\\s-]\", \" \", line).strip()\n    62\u2192                body_clean = re.sub(r\"\\s+\", \" \", body_clean)\n    63\u2192                body_words = body_clean.split()\n    64\u2192                if len(body_words) >= 3:\n    65\u2192                    add(\" \".join(body_words[:6]))\n    66\u2192                    break\n    67\u2192\n None,\n    75\u2192    body: str = \"\",\n    76\u2192) -> dict:\n    77\u2192    \"\"\"Run a full prior-art scan and return structured results.\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192    Args:\n    80\u2192        idea: The idea description to scan.\n    81\u2192        github_token: GitHub personal access token (required for GitHub source).\n    82\u2192        sources: List of source names to query. Defaults to [\"github\", \"hn\"].\n    83\u2192        body: Optional body text for additional query extraction.\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192    Returns:\n    86\u2192        Structured dict: {query, queries, sources, signal, meta}\n    87\u2192    \"\"\"\n    88\u2192    if sources is None:\n    89\u2192        sources = [\"github\", \"hn\"]\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192    queries = build_queries(idea, body)\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192    all_repos: list[dict] = []\n    94\u2192    all_hn: list[dict] = []\n    95\u2192    seen_repo_names: set[str] = set()\n    96\u2192    seen_hn_urls: set[str] = set()\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192    for query in queries:\n    99\u2192        if \"github\" in sources:\n   100\u2192            repos = search_repos(query, github_token)\n   101\u2192            for r in repos:\n   102\u2192                if r[\"name\"] not in seen_repo_names:\n   103\u2192                    seen_repo_names.add(r[\"name\"])\n   104\u2192                    all_repos.append(r)\n   105\u2192            time.sleep(1)\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192        if \"hn\" in sources:\n   108\u2192            hn = search_discussions(query)\n   109\u2192            for h in hn:\n   110\u2192                if h[\"url\"] not in seen_hn_urls:\n   111\u2192                    seen_hn_urls.add(h[\"url\"])\n   112\u2192                    all_hn.append(h)\n   113\u2192            time.sleep(0.5)\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192    all_repos.sort(key=lambda r: r[\"stars\"], reverse=True)\n   116\u2192    all_hn.sort(key=lambda h: h[\"points\"], reverse=True)\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    signal = compute_signal(all_repos, all_hn)\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192    source_results = []\n   121\u2192    if \"github\" in sources:\n   122\u2192        source_results.append({\"type\": \"github\", \"results\": all_repos})\n   123\u2192    if \"hn\" in sources:\n   124\u2192        source_results.append({\"type\": \"hn\", \"results\": all_hn})\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192    return {\n   127\u2192        \"query\": idea,\n   128\u2192        \"queries\": queries,\n   129\u2192        \"sources\": source_results,\n   130\u2192        \"signal\": signal,\n   131\u2192        \"meta\": {\n   132\u2192            \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n   133\u2192            \"version\": __version__,\n   134\u2192        },\n   135\u2192    }\n   136\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

     1→[build-system]
     2→requires = ["hatchling"]
     3→build-backend = "hatchling.build"
     4→
     5→[project]
     6→name = "priorart"
     7→version = "0.1.1"
     8→description = "Prior art scanner — search GitHub and HN for existing solutions to an idea."
     9→readme = "README.md"
    10→license = "MIT"
    11→requires-python = ">=3.11"
    12→authors = [{ name = "Kim" }]
    13→keywords = ["prior-art", "github", "hackernews", "research", "devtools"]
    14→classifiers = [
    15→    "Development Status :: 3 - Alpha",
    16→    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
    17→    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",
    18→    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
    19→    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
    20→    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
    21→]
    22→dependencies = [
    23→    "httpx>=0.27",
    24→    "typer>=0.12",
    25→]
    26→
    27→[project.optional-dependencies]
    28→dev = [
    29→    "pytest>=8.0",
    30→    "pytest-cov>=5.0",
    31→    "ruff>=0.4",
    32→]
    33→
    34→[project.scripts]
    35→priorart = "priorart.cli:app"
    36→
    37→[project.urls]
    38→Homepage = "https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/priorart"
    39→Repository = "https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/priorart"
    40→
    41→[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
    42→packages = ["src/priorart"]
    43→
    44→[tool.pytest.ini_options]
    45→testpaths = ["tests"]
    46→
    47→[tool.ruff]
    48→target-version = "py311"
    49→
    50→[tool.ruff.lint]
    51→select = ["E", "F", "I"]
    52→

USERMESSAGE #24
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→"""CLI entry point for priorart."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→from typing import Annotated
     6→
     7→import typer
     8→
     9→from priorart.formatters import format_json, format_markdown, format_table
    10→from priorart.scanner import scan
    11→
    12→app = typer.Typer(
    13→    name="priorart",
    14→    help="Scan GitHub and Hacker News for prior art on an idea.",
    15→    add_completion=False,
    16→)
    17→
    18→
    19→@app.callback(invoke_without_command=True)
    20→def main(ctx: typer.Context) -> None:
    21→    """Prior art scanner — search GitHub and HN for existing solutions."""
    22→    if ctx.invoked_subcommand is None:
    23→        typer.echo(ctx.get_help())
    24→        raise typer.Exit()
    25→
    26→
    27→@app.command("scan")
    28→def scan_cmd(
    29→    idea: Annotated[str, typer.Argument(help="Idea description to scan")],
    30→    format: Annotated[
    31→        str,
    32→        typer.Option(
    33→            "--format",
    34→            "-f",
    35→            help="Output format: json, markdown, or table",
    36→        ),
    37→    ] = "table",
    38→    sources: Annotated[
    39→        str,
    40→        typer.Option(
    41→            "--sources",
    42→            "-s",
    43→            help="Comma-separated sources to query (github,hn)",
    44→        ),
    45→    ] = "github,hn",
    46→    github_token: Annotated[
    47→        str | None,
    48→        typer.Option(
    49→            "--github-token",
    50→            envvar="GITHUB_TOKEN",
    51→            help="GitHub personal access token (or set GITHUB_TOKEN env var)",
    52→            show_default=False,
    53→        ),
    54→    ] = None,
    55→) -> None:
    56→    """Scan for prior art on an idea."""
    57→    fmt = format.lower().strip()
    58→    if fmt not in ("json", "markdown", "table"):
    59→        typer.echo(
    60→            f"Error: invalid format '{fmt}'. Choose from: json, markdown, table",
    61→            err=True,
    62→        )
    63→        raise typer.Exit(code=1)
    64→
    65→    source_list = [s.strip().lower() for s in sources.split(",") if s.strip()]
    66→    for s in source_list:
    67→        if s not in ("github", "hn"):
    68→            typer.echo(
    69→                f"Error: unknown source '{s}'. Choose from: github, hn",
    70→                err=True,
    71→            )
    72→            raise typer.Exit(code=1)
    73→
    74→    if "github" in source_list and not github_token:
    75→        typer.echo(
    76→            "Note: No GITHUB_TOKEN set. GitHub search will work but is "
    77→            "rate-limited (10 req/min). Set GITHUB_TOKEN for higher limits.",
    78→            err=True,
    79→        )
    80→
    81→    result = scan(idea, github_token=github_token, sources=source_list)
    82→
    83→    if fmt == "json":
    84→        typer.echo(format_json(result))
    85→    elif fmt == "markdown":
    86→        typer.echo(format_markdown(result))
    87→    else:
    88→        typer.echo(format_table(result))
    89→
    90→
    91→if __name__ == "__main__":
    92→    app()
    93→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

 12\u2192app = typer.Typer(\n    13\u2192    name=\"priorart\",\n    14\u2192    help=\"Scan GitHub and Hacker News for prior art on an idea.\",\n    15\u2192    add_completion=False,\n    16\u2192)\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192@app.callback(invoke_without_command=True)\n    20\u2192def main(ctx: typer.Context) -> None:\n    21\u2192    \"\"\"Prior art scanner \u2014 search GitHub and 54\u2192    ] = None,\n    55\u2192) -> None:\n    56\u2192    \"\"\"Scan for prior art on an idea.\"\"\"\n    57\u2192    fmt = format.lower().strip()\n    58\u2192    if fmt not in (\"json\", \"markdown\", \"table\"):\n    59\u2192        typer.echo(\n    60\u2192            f\"Error: invalid format '{fmt}'. Choose from: json, markdown, table\",\n    61\u2192            err=True,\n    62\u2192        )\n    63\u2192        raise typer.Exit(code=1)\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192    source_list = [s.strip().lower() for s in sources.split(\",\") if s.strip()]\n    66\u2192    for s in source_list:\n    67\u2192        if s not in (\"github\", \"hn\"):\n    68\u2192            typer.echo(\n    69\u2192                f\"Error: unknown source '{s}'. Choose from: github, hn\",\n    70\u2192                err=True,\n    71\u2192            )\n    72\u2192            raise typer.Exit(code=1)\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192    if \"github\" in source_list and not github_token:\n    75\u2192        typer.echo(\n    76\u2192            \"Note: No GITHUB_TOKEN set. GitHub search will work but is \"\n    77\u2192            \"rate-limited (10 req/min). Set GITHUB_TOKEN for higher limits.\",\n    78\u2192            err=True,\n    79\u2192        )\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192    result = scan(idea, github_token=github_token, sources=source_list)\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192    if fmt == \"json\":\n    84\u2192        typer.echo(format_json(result))\n    85\u2192    elif fmt == \"markdown\":\n    86\u2192        typer.echo(format_markdown(result))\n    87\u2192    else:\n    88\u2192        typer.echo(format_table(result))\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    92\u2192    app()\n    93\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. ✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→"""Tests for scoring module."""
     2→
     3→
     4→from priorart.scoring import compute_signal
     5→
     6→
     7→def make_repo(stars: int) -> dict:
     8→    return {
     9→        "name": "org/repo",
    10→        "stars": stars,
    11→        "description": "test",
    12→        "url": "https://github.com/org/repo",
    13→        "updated": "2024-01-01",
    14→        "language": "Python",
    15→    }
    16→
    17→
    18→def make_hn(points: int) -> dict:
    19→    return {
    20→        "title": "Test post",
    21→        "points": points,
    22→        "comments": 10,
    23→        "url": "https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=1",
    24→        "date": "2024-01-01",
    25→    }
    26→
    27→
    28→class TestComputeSignal:
    29→    def test_empty_results_returns_zero(self):
    30→        result = compute_signal([], [])
    31→        assert result["score"] == 0
    32→        assert result["verdict"] == "LOW"
    33→        assert result["breakdown"]["github"] == 0
    34→        assert result["breakdown"]["hn"] == 0
    35→
    36→    def test_low_verdict_under_30(self):
    37→        result = compute_signal([], [])
    38→        assert result["verdict"] == "LOW"
    39→        assert result["score"] < 30
    40→
    41→    def test_moderate_verdict_30_to_60(self):
    42→        # 4 repos with low stars → gh=30, no HN → score=30 → MODERATE
    43→        repos = [make_repo(10) for _ in range(4)]
    44→        result = compute_signal(repos, [])
    45→        assert result["verdict"] == "MODERATE"
    46→        assert 30 <= result["score"] <= 60
    47→
    48→    def test_high_verdict_over_60(self):
    49→        # 8+ repos with 5000+ stars + 3+ HN posts → HIGH
    50→        repos = [make_repo(10000) for _ in range(10)]
    51→        hn = [make_hn(200) for _ in range(5)]
    52→        result = compute_signal(repos, hn)
    53→        assert result["verdict"] == "HIGH"
    54→        assert result["score"] > 60
    55→
    56→    def test_max_stars_boost_github(self):
    57→        one_repo = [make_repo(5000)]
    58→        result = compute_signal(one_repo, [])
    59→        # 1 repo = 15, 5000 stars = +20 → 35
    60→        assert result["breakdown"]["github"] == 35
    61→
    62→    def test_score_capped_at_100(self):
    63→        repos = [make_repo(10000) for _ in range(20)]
    64→        hn = [make_hn(500) for _ in range(20)]
    65→        result = compute_signal(repos, hn)
    66→        assert result["score"] <= 100
    67→
    68→    def test_breakdown_keys_present(self):
    69→        result = compute_signal([], [])
    70→        assert "github" in result["breakdown"]
    71→        assert "hn" in result["breakdown"]
    72→
    73→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→    # GitHub tier boundaries
    75→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    76→
    77→    def test_gh_score_single_repo_no_stars(self):
    78→        result = compute_signal([make_repo(10)], [])
    79→        # 1 repo (base=15); 10 stars → no boost
    80→        assert result["breakdown"]["github"] == 15
    81→
    82→    def test_gh_score_4_repos_no_stars(self):
    83→        result = compute_signal([make_repo(10) for _ in range(4)], [])
    84→        # 4 repos (base=30); 10 stars → no boost
    85→        assert result["breakdown"]["github"] == 30
    86→
    87→    def test_gh_score_8_repos_no_stars(self):
    88→        result = compute_signal([make_repo(10) for _ in range(8)], [])
    89→        # 8 repos (base=45); 10 stars → no boost
    90→        assert result["breakdown"]["github"] == 45
    91→
    92→    def test_gh_stars_100_to_999_adds_10(self):
    93→        result = compute_signal([make_repo(500)], [])
    94→        # base=15; 500 stars (100–999) → +10 = 25
    95→        assert result["breakdown"]["github"] == 25
    96→
    97→    def test_gh_stars_1000_to_4999_adds_15(self):
    98→        result = compute_signal([make_repo(2000)], [])
    99→        # base=15; 2000 stars (1000–4999) → +15 = 30
   100→        assert result["breakdown"]["github"] == 30
   101→
   102→    def test_gh_score_capped_at_60(self):
   103→        result = compute_signal([make_repo(10000) for _ in range(10)], [])
   104→        # base=45; 10000 stars → +20 = 65, capped at 60
   105→        assert result["breakdown"]["github"] == 60
   106→
   107→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   108→    # HN tier boundaries
   109→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   110→
   111→    def test_hn_score_2_posts_no_boost(self):
   112→        result = compute_signal([], [make_hn(5), make_hn(10)])
   113→        # 2 posts (base=10); max_points=10 < 30 → no boost
   114→        assert result["breakdown"]["hn"] == 10
   115→
   116→    def test_hn_score_3_posts_no_boost(self):
   117→        result = compute_signal([], [make_hn(5) for _ in range(3)])
   118→        # 3 posts (base=25); max_points=5 < 30 → no boost
   119→        assert result["breakdown"]["hn"] == 25
   120→
   121→    def test_hn_points_30_to_99_adds_10(self):
   122→        result = compute_signal([], [make_hn(50)])
   123→        # 1 post (base=10); 50 pts (30–99) → +10 = 20
   124→        assert result["breakdown"]["hn"] == 20
   125→
   126→    def test_hn_points_100_plus_adds_15(self):
   127→        result = compute_signal([], [make_hn(150)])
   128→        # 1 post (base=10); 150 pts (≥100) → +15 = 25
   129→        assert result["breakdown"]["hn"] == 25
   130→
   131→    def test_hn_score_capped_at_40(self):
   132→        result = compute_signal([], [make_hn(500) for _ in range(5)])
   133→        # 5 posts (base=25); 500 pts → +15 = 40, capped at 40
   134→        assert result["breakdown"]["hn"] == 40
   135→
   136→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   137→    # Verdict boundary values
   138→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   139→
   140→    def test_score_exactly_30_is_moderate(self):
   141→        # 4 repos, 10 stars → gh=30, hn=0 → score=30 → MODERATE
   142→        result = compute_signal([make_repo(10) for _ in range(4)], [])
   143→        assert result["score"] == 30
   144→        assert result["verdict"] == "MODERATE"
   145→
   146→    def test_score_25_is_low(self):
   147→        # 1 repo, 500 stars → gh=15+10=25, hn=0 → score=25 → LOW
   148→        result = compute_signal([make_repo(500)], [])
   149→        assert result["score"] == 25
   150→        assert result["verdict"] == "LOW"
   151→
   152→    def test_score_exactly_60_is_moderate(self):
   153→        # 8 repos, 2000 stars → gh=45+15=60, hn=0 → score=60 → MODERATE
   154→        result = compute_signal([make_repo(2000) for _ in range(8)], [])
   155→        assert result["score"] == 60
   156→        assert result["verdict"] == "MODERATE"
   157→
   158→    def test_score_above_60_is_high(self):
   159→        # 8 repos (500 stars) + 1 HN post (20 pts) → gh=55, hn=10 → score=65 → HIGH
   160→        result = compute_signal(
   161→            [make_repo(500) for _ in range(8)],
   162→            [make_hn(20)],
   163→        )
   164→        assert result["score"] == 65
   165→        assert result["verdict"] == "HIGH"
   166→

     1→"""Tests for scanner module — query building and scan orchestration."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import unittest.mock
     6→from unittest.mock import patch
     7→
     8→from priorart.scanner import build_queries, scan
     9→
    10→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    11→# Shared fixtures for TestScan
    12→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    13→
    14→_MOCK_REPO = {
    15→    "name": "org/repo",
    16→    "stars": 100,
    17→    "description": "A test repo",
    18→    "url": "https://github.com/org/repo",
    19→    "updated": "2024-01-01",
    20→    "language": "Python",
    21→}
    22→
    23→_MOCK_HN_POST = {
    24→    "title": "Test HN post",
    25→    "points": 50,
    26→    "comments": 5,
    27→    "url": "https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=1",
    28→    "date": "2024-01-01",
    29→}
    30→
    31→
    32→class TestBuildQueries:
    33→    def test_simple_idea(self):
    34→        queries = build_queries("AI personal finance tracker")
    35→        assert len(queries) >= 1
    36→        assert "AI personal finance tracker" in queries
    37→
    38→    def test_strips_idea_prefix(self):
    39→        queries = build_queries("idea: AI personal finance tracker")
    40→        for q in queries:
    41→            assert not q.lower().startswith("idea:")
    42→
    43→    def test_strips_idea_bracket_prefix(self):
    44→        queries = build_queries("[idea] AI personal finance tracker")
    45→        for q in queries:
    46→            assert not q.lower().startswith("[idea]")
    47→
    48→    def test_splits_on_dash_separator(self):
    49→        queries = build_queries("Kuma — Self-hosted uptime monitor")
    50→        # Should produce multi-word queries, not just "Kuma"
    51→        assert any(len(q.split()) > 1 for q in queries)
    52→
    53→    def test_splits_on_em_dash(self):
    54→        queries = build_queries("FinTrack — AI finance tracker")
    55→        assert any("AI finance tracker" in q or "finance tracker" in q for q in queries)
    56→
    57→    def test_long_title_adds_shortened_variants(self):
    58→        long_title = "AI-powered personal finance tracking application for mobile"
    59→        queries = build_queries(long_title)
    60→        # Should add 5-word and 3-word variants
    61→        assert len(queries) >= 2
    62→
    63→    def test_no_duplicate_queries(self):
    64→        queries = build_queries("AI personal finance tracker")
    65→        lower = [q.lower() for q in queries]
    66→        assert len(lower) == len(set(lower))
    67→
    68→    def test_body_adds_extra_query(self):
    69→        queries = build_queries(
    70→            "Finance app",
    71→            body="Smart budgeting with machine learning insights",
    72→        )
    73→        assert any("Smart budgeting" in q or "budgeting" in q for q in queries)
    74→
    75→    def test_body_ignores_http_lines(self):
    76→        queries = build_queries(
    77→            "Finance app",
    78→            body="https://example.com/something
Smart budgeting tool",
    79→        )
    80→        # URL line should be skipped
    81→        for q in queries:
    82→            assert "https://" not in q
    83→
    84→    def test_returns_list_of_strings(self):
    85→        queries = build_queries("test idea")
    86→        assert isinstance(queries, list)
    87→        assert all(isinstance(q, str) for q in queries)
    88→
    89→    def test_empty_title_edge_case(self):
    90→        # Should not raise, returns whatever makes sense
    91→        queries = build_queries("   ")
    92→        assert isinstance(queries, list)
    93→
    94→    def test_strips_idea_dash_prefix(self):
    95→        queries = build_queries("idea - AI personal finance tracker")
    96→        for q in queries:
    97→            assert not q.lower().startswith("idea")
    98→
    99→    def test_pipe_separator_splits(self):
   100→        queries = build_queries("FinTrack | AI finance tracker")
   101→        assert any(len(q.split()) > 1 for q in queries)
   102→
   103→
   104→class TestScan:
   105→    """Tests for the scan() orchestrator function."""
   106→
   107→    def _scan(
   108→        self,
   109→        idea="test idea",
   110→        token="fake-token",
   111→        sources=None,
   112→        repos=None,
   113→        hn=None,
   114→        **kwargs,
   115→    ):
   116→        """Helper: run scan() with mocked sources and no sleeps."""
   117→        repos = [] if repos is None else repos
   118→        hn = [] if hn is None else hn
   119→        sources = sources if sources is not None else ["github", "hn"]
   120→        with patch("priorart.scanner.search_repos", return_value=repos):
   121→            with patch("priorart.scanner.search_discussions", return_value=hn):
   122→                with patch("priorart.scanner.time.sleep"):
   123→                    return scan(idea, github_token=token, sources=sources, **kwargs)
   124→
   125→    def test_returns_required_structure(self):
   126→        result = self._scan()
   127→        assert "query" in result
   128→        assert "queries" in result
   129→        assert "sources" in result
   130→        assert "signal" in result
   131→        assert "meta" in result
   132→
   133→    def test_query_field_matches_input(self):
   134→        result = self._scan(idea="my unique idea")
   135→        assert result["query"] == "my unique idea"
   136→
   137→    def test_queries_is_list_of_strings(self):
   138→        result = self._scan()
   139→        assert isinstance(result["queries"], list)
   140→        assert all(isinstance(q, str) for q in result["queries"])
   141→
   142→    def test_calls_github_without_token(self):
   143→        with patch("priorart.scanner.search_repos", return_value=[]) as mock_repos:
   144→            with patch("priorart.scanner.search_discussions", return_value=[]):
   145→                with patch("priorart.scanner.time.sleep"):
   146→                    scan("test idea", github_token=None, sources=["github", "hn"])
   147→        assert mock_repos.called
   148→        mock_repos.assert_called_with(unittest.mock.ANY, None)
   149→
   150→    def test_calls_github_with_empty_token(self):
   151→        with patch("priorart.scanner.search_repos", return_value=[]) as mock_repos:
   152→            with patch("priorart.scanner.search_discussions", return_value=[]):
   153→                with patch("priorart.scanner.time.sleep"):
   154→                    scan("test idea", github_token="", sources=["github", "hn"])
   155→        assert mock_repos.called
   156→
   157→    def test_calls_github_when_token_provided(self):
   158→        with patch("priorart.scanner.search_repos", return_value=[]) as mock_repos:
   159→            with patch("priorart.scanner.search_discussions", return_value=[]):
   160→                with patch("priorart.scanner.time.sleep"):
   161→                    scan("test idea", github_token="fake-token", sources=["github"])
   162→        assert mock_repos.called
   163→
   164→    def test_calls_hn_when_in_sources(self):
   165→        with patch("priorart.scanner.search_repos", return_value=[]):
   166→            with patch(
   167→                "priorart.scanner.search_discussions", return_value=[]
   168→            ) as mock_hn:
   169→                with patch("priorart.scanner.time.sleep"):
   170→                    scan("test idea", github_token=None, sources=["hn"])
   171→        assert mock_hn.called
   172→
   173→    def test_skips_hn_when_not_in_sources(self):
   174→        with patch("priorart.scanner.search_repos", return_value=[]):
   175→            with patch("priorart.scanner.search_discussions") as mock_hn:
   176→                with patch("priorart.scanner.time.sleep"):
   177→                    scan("test idea", github_token="fake-token", sources=["github"])
   178→        mock_hn.assert_not_called()
   179→
   180→    def test_deduplicates_repos_by_name(self):
   181→        shared = {**_MOCK_REPO, "name": "org/shared"}
   182→        unique = {**_MOCK_REPO, "name": "org/unique"}
   183→        call_n = [0]
   184→
   185→        def _repos(query, token, **kw):
   186→            call_n[0] += 1
   187→            return [shared] if call_n[0] == 1 else [shared, unique]
   188→
   189→        # Use a long title so build_queries produces 2+ queries
   190→        with patch("priorart.scanner.search_repos", side_effect=_repos):
   191→            with patch("priorart.scanner.search_discussions", return_value=[]):
   192→                with patch("priorart.scanner.time.sleep"):
   193→                    result = scan(
   194→                        "AI-powered personal finance tracking application for mobile",
   195→                        github_token="fake-token",
   196→                        sources=["github"],
   197→                    )
   198→
   199→        gh_results = next(
   200→            s["results"] for s in result["sources"] if s["type"] == "github"
   201→        )
   202→        names = [r["name"] for r in gh_results]
   203→        assert names.count("org/shared") == 1
   204→
   205→    def test_deduplicates_hn_by_url(self):
   206→        dup = {**_MOCK_HN_POST, "url": "https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=42"}
   207→        # Return same post for every query
   208→        with patch("priorart.scanner.search_repos", return_value=[]):
   209→            with patch("priorart.scanner.search_discussions", return_value=[dup]):
   210→                with patch("priorart.scanner.time.sleep"):
   211→                    result = scan(
   212→                        "AI-powered personal finance tracking application for mobile",
   213→                        github_token=None,
   214→                        sources=["hn"],
   215→                    )
   216→        hn_results = next(s["results"] for s in result["sources"] if s["type"] == "hn")
   217→        urls = [r["url"] for r in hn_results]
   218→        assert len(urls) == len(set(urls))
   219→
   220→    def test_repos_sorted_by_stars_descending(self):
   221→        low = {**_MOCK_REPO, "name": "org/low", "stars": 10}
   222→        high = {**_MOCK_REPO, "name": "org/high", "stars": 1000}
   223→        result = self._scan(repos=[low, high])
   224→        gh = next(s["results"] for s in result["sources"] if s["type"] == "github")
   225→        stars = [r["stars"] for r in gh]
   226→        assert stars == sorted(stars, reverse=True)
   227→
   228→    def test_hn_sorted_by_points_descending(self):
   229→        low = {
   230→            **_MOCK_HN_POST,
   231→            "url": "https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=1",
   232→            "points": 5,
   233→        }
   234→        high = {
   235→            **_MOCK_HN_POST,
   236→            "url": "https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=2",
   237→            "points": 500,
   238→        }
   239→        result = self._scan(hn=[low, high], token=None, sources=["hn"])
   240→        hn = next(s["results"] for s in result["sources"] if s["type"] == "hn")
   241→        pts = [r["points"] for r in hn]
   242→        assert pts == sorted(pts, reverse=True)
   243→
   244→    def test_meta_timestamp_is_iso_format(self):
   245→        result = self._scan()
   246→        ts = result["meta"]["timestamp"]
   247→        assert "T" in ts
   248→        assert "+" in ts or "Z" in ts  # timezone-aware
   249→
   250→    def test_meta_version_is_string(self):
   251→        result = self._scan()
   252→        assert isinstance(result["meta"]["version"], str)
   253→        assert len(result["meta"]["version"]) > 0
   254→
   255→    def test_source_types_reflect_requested_sources(self):
   256→        result = self._scan(sources=["hn"], token=None)
   257→        types = [s["type"] for s in result["sources"]]
   258→        assert types == ["hn"]
   259→
   260→    def test_github_source_absent_when_not_requested(self):
   261→        result = self._scan(sources=["hn"], token=None)
   262→        types = [s["type"] for s in result["sources"]]
   263→        assert "github" not in types
   264→
   265→    def test_both_source_types_present_by_default(self):
   266→        result = self._scan()
   267→        types = [s["type"] for s in result["sources"]]
   268→        assert "github" in types
   269→        assert "hn" in types
   270→
   271→    def test_signal_score_reflects_results(self):
   272→        repos = [
   273→            {**_MOCK_REPO, "name": f"org/repo{i}", "stars": 5000} for i in range(8)
   274→        ]
   275→        result = self._scan(repos=repos)
   276→        assert result["signal"]["score"] > 0
   277→
   278→    def test_empty_results_give_zero_signal(self):
   279→        result = self._scan(token=None, sources=["hn"])
   280→        assert result["signal"]["score"] == 0
   281→        assert result["signal"]["verdict"] == "LOW"
   282→
   283→    def test_body_param_influences_queries(self):
   284→        """Additional body text should expand the query list."""
   285→        with patch("priorart.scanner.search_repos", return_value=[]):
   286→            with patch("priorart.scanner.search_discussions", return_value=[]):
   287→                with patch("priorart.scanner.time.sleep"):
   288→                    result_no_body = scan(
   289→                        "Finance app", github_token=None, sources=["hn"]
   290→                    )
   291→                    result_with_body = scan(
   292→                        "Finance app",
   293→                        github_token=None,
   294→                        sources=["hn"],
   295→                        body="Smart budgeting with machine learning insights",
   296→                    )
   297→        assert len(result_with_body["queries"]) >= len(result_no_body["queries"])
   298→

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #29
Now I understand the codebase. The issue FRG-255 asks me to: 1. Embed the idea description and each result's description/title 2. Rank results by cosine similarity 3. Use an embedding API (OpenAI embeddings or similar) 4. Results that are conceptually similar rank higher even without shared keywords Key acceptance criteria: - Results are reordered by semantic relevance - A search for "document formatter" surfaces PDF/formatting tools even if they don't contain those exact words - Signal score incorporates semantic similarity The current flow in scanner.py: 1. Build queries 2. Fetch repos and HN posts 3. Sort by stars/points 4. Compute signal I need to add semantic similarity ranking between the idea description and each result. OpenAI's embedding API seems like the right choice since it's widely used. I'll need to add an embedding function, compute cosine similarity scores, and integrate this into the existing ranking pipeline to reorder results by semantic relevance before the final signal computation. For the implementation, I'm thinking of creating a separate embeddings module to handle API calls and similarity calculations, then updating the scanner to optionally rerank using these scores. The OpenAI API key should be optional so the tool degrades gracefully if it's not provided, and I'll need to add a CLI flag to accept it along with updating the project dependencies. , then incorporate semantic similarity into the scoring logic to weight results based on relevance. Looking at the acceptance criteria, the main requirement is reordering results by semantic relevance, so I should focus on that as the primary mechanism rather than just boosting an existing signal score. I'm realizing the signal score should incorporate semantic similarity more directly—maybe by adding a boost when top results have high similarity scores, or by filtering the signal computation to only count semantically relevant results rather than all results found. The key is keeping this simple and intuitive rather than making the signal behave unpredictably. From a product angle, finding multiple PDF tools when searching "document formatter" represents real competition, whereas finding unrelated tools doesn't. So weighting the signal by semantic relevance makes sense. Writing the embedding module... Writing the similarity ranking logic... Now I'm thinking about how to integrate semantic scoring into the signal calculation. If an OpenAI key is provided, I can compute a semantic component based on the average similarity of the top results and add it to the signal breakdown with a reasonable cap. I'm going in circles here—let me step back and think about what actually matters. The key insight is that semantic similarity should boost the score for results that are genuinely relevant, so I should weight the top matches more heavily. I'll compute the average similarity across the top 5 results from each source and scale that to a 0-20 point boost, keeping the total capped at 100. Writing the scoring function... I need to implement the embedding pipeline in `scanner.py`: if an OpenAI key is provided, I'll embed the idea description and then embed each result's text (repo names with descriptions, HN titles), compute similarity scores, and calculate the average of the top-3 similarities to pass to `compute_signal()`. I should also add the OpenAI key as an optional CLI parameter and think through the test coverage for this new functionality.

Results that are conceptually similar rank higher even without shared keywords\n\nKey acceptance criteria:\n- Results are reordered by semantic relevance\n- A search for \"document formatter\" surfaces PDF/formatting tools even if they don't contain those exact words\n- Signal score incorporates semantic similarity\n\nThe current flow in scanner.py:\n1. Build queries\n2. Fetch repos and HN posts\n\n\n3. Sort by stars/points\n4. Compute signal\n\nI need to add semantic similarity ranking between the idea description and each result. OpenAI's embedding API seems like the right choice since it's widely used. I'll need to add an embedding function, compute cosine similarity scores, and integrate this into the existing ranking pipeline to reorder results by semantic relevance before the final signal computation.\n\nFor the implementation, I'm thinking of creating a separate embeddings module to handle API calls and similarity calculations, then updating the scanner to optionally rerank using these scores. The OpenAI API key should be optional so the tool degrades gracefully if it's not provided, and I'll need to add a CLI flag to accept it along with updating the project dependencies. , then incorporate semantic similarity into the scoring logic to weight results based on relevance. Looking at the acceptance criteria, the main requirement is reordering results by semantic relevance, so I should focus on that as the primary mechanism rather than just boosting an existing signal score. I'm realizing the signal score should incorporate semantic similarity more directly\u2014maybe by adding a boost when top results have high similarity scores, or by filtering the signal computation to only count semantically relevant results rather than all results found. The key is keeping this simple and intuitive rather than making the signal behave unpredictably.\n\nFrom a product angle, finding multiple PDF tools when searching \"document formatter\" represents real competition, whereas finding unrelated tools doesn't. So weighting the signal by semantic relevance makes sense.\n\nWriting the embedding module... Writing the similarity ranking logic...\n\nNow I'm thinking about how to integrate semantic scoring into the signal calculation. If an OpenAI key is provided, I can compute a semantic component based on the average similarity of the top results and add it to the signal breakdown with a reasonable cap. I'm going in circles here\u2014let me step back and think about what actually matters. The key insight is that semantic similarity should boost the score for results that are genuinely relevant, so I should weight the top matches more heavily. I'll compute the average similarity across the top 5 results from each source and scale that to a 0-20 point boost, keeping the total capped at 100.\n\nWriting the scoring function... I need to implement the embedding pipeline in `scanner.py`: if an OpenAI key is provided, I'll embed the idea description and then embed each result's text (repo names with descriptions, HN titles), compute similarity scores, and calculate the average of the top-3 similarities to pass to `compute_signal()`. I should also add the OpenAI key as an optional CLI parameter and think through the test coverage for this new functionality.",

     5→import json
     6→
     7→
     8→def format_json(result: dict) -> str:
     9→    """Format scan result as pretty-printed JSON."""
    10→    return json.dumps(result, indent=2)
    11→
    12→
    13→def format_markdown(result: dict) -> str:
    14→    """Format scan result as a GitHub-style Markdown comment."""
    15→    signal = result["signal"]
    16→    score = signal["score"]
    17→    verdict = signal["verdict"]
    18→    queries = result.get("queries", [])
    19→
    20→    verdict_labels = {
    21→        "LOW": "🟢 LOW — Greenfield opportunity or underserved niche",
    22→        "MODERATE": "🟡 MODERATE — Some existing solutions, differentiation needed",
    23→        "HIGH": "🔴 HIGH — Crowded space, needs strong differentiator",
    24→    }
    25→    verdict_str = verdict_labels.get(verdict, verdict)
    26→
    27→    lines = [
    28→        "## 🔍 Prior Art Scan",
    29→        "",
    30→        f"**Reality Signal:** {score}/100 — {verdict_str}",
    31→        f"**Queries:** {', '.join(f'`{q}`' for q in queries)}",
    32→        "",
    33→    ]
    34→
    35→    # GitHub repos
    36→    repos = next(
    37→        (s["results"] for s in result.get("sources", []) if s["type"] == "github"),
    38→        [],
    39→    )
    40→    lines.append("### GitHub Repositories")
    41→    if repos:
    42→        lines.append("")
    43→        lines.append("| Repo | ⭐ | Language | Description |")
    44→        lines.append("|------|-----|----------|-------------|")
    45→        for r in repos[:8]:
    46→            desc = (r.get("description") or "—").replace("|", "\\|")
    47→            row = (
    48→                f"| [{r['name']}]({r['url']}) | {r['stars']:,}"
    49→                f" | {r['language']} | {desc} |"
    50→            )
    51→            lines.append(row)
    52→    else:
    53→        lines.append("No relevant repositories found.")
    54→    lines.append("")
    55→
    56→    # HN discussions
    57→    hn_posts = next(
    58→        (s["results"] for s in result.get("sources", []) if s["type"] == "hn"),
    59→        [],
    60→    )
    61→    lines.append("### Hacker News Discussions")
    62→    if hn_posts:
    63→        lines.append("")
    64→        for p in hn_posts:
    65→            lines.append(
    66→                f"- [{p['title']}]({p['url']}) — {p['points']} pts, "
    67→                f"{p['comments']} comments ({p['date']})"
    68→            )
    69→    else:
    70→        lines.append("No relevant discussions found.")
    71→    lines.append("")
    72→
    73→    lines.append("---")
    74→    lines.append(
    75→        "*Auto-generated by priorart. Data informs Founder and attack agents.*"
    76→    )
    77→
    78→    return "
".join(lines)
    79→
    80→
    81→def format_table(result: dict) -> str:
    82→    """Format scan result as plain-text tables."""
    83→    signal = result["signal"]
    84→    score = signal["score"]
    85→    verdict = signal["verdict"]
    86→    queries = result.get("queries", [])
    87→
    88→    lines = [
    89→        "Prior Art Scan",
    90→        "=" * 60,
    91→        f"Reality Signal: {score}/100  ({verdict})",
    92→        f"Queries: {', '.join(queries)}",
    93→        "",
    94→    ]
    95→
    96→    # GitHub repos
    97→    repos = next(
    98→        (s["results"] for s in result.get("sources", []) if s["type"] == "github"),
    99→        [],
   100→    )
   101→    lines.append("GitHub Repositories")
   102→    lines.append("-" * 60)
   103→    if repos:
   104→        col_widths = {"name": 35, "stars": 8, "language": 12, "description": 40}
   105→        header = (
   106→            f"{'Repo':<{col_widths['name']}} "
   107→            f"{'Stars':>{col_widths['stars']}} "
   108→            f"{'Language':<{col_widths['language']}} "
   109→            f"Description"
   110→        )
   111→        lines.append(header)
   112→        lines.append("-" * 60)
   113→        for r in repos[:8]:
   114→            name = r["name"][: col_widths["name"]]
   115→            desc = (r.get("description") or "—")[: col_widths["description"]]
   116→            lang = (r.get("language") or "—")[: col_widths["language"]]
   117→            lines.append(
   118→                f"{name:<{col_widths['name']}} "
   119→                f"{r['stars']:>{col_widths['stars']},} "
   120→                f"{lang:<{col_widths['language']}} "
   121→                f"{desc}"
   122→            )
   123→    else:
   124→        lines.append("No relevant repositories found.")
   125→    lines.append("")
   126→
   127→    # HN discussions
   128→    hn_posts = next(
   129→        (s["results"] for s in result.get("sources", []) if s["type"] == "hn"),
   130→        [],
   131→    )
   132→    lines.append("Hacker News Discussions")
   133→    lines.append("-" * 60)
   134→    if hn_posts:
   135→        for p in hn_posts:
   136→            title = p["title"][:55]
   137→            lines.append(f"  {title}")
   138→            lines.append(
   139→                f"    {p['points']} pts, {p['comments']} comments  {p['date']}"
   140→            )
   141→            lines.append(f"    {p['url']}")
   142→    else:
   143→        lines.append("No relevant discussions found.")
   144→
   145→    return "
".join(lines)
   146→

     1→"""Tests for formatters module."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→
     5→from priorart.formatters import format_json, format_markdown, format_table
     6→
     7→SAMPLE_RESULT = {
     8→    "query": "AI personal finance tracker",
     9→    "queries": ["AI personal finance tracker", "AI personal finance"],
    10→    "sources": [
    11→        {
    12→            "type": "github",
    13→            "results": [
    14→                {
    15→                    "name": "org/finance-ai",
    16→                    "stars": 1234,
    17→                    "description": "AI-powered finance tracker",
    18→                    "url": "https://github.com/org/finance-ai",
    19→                    "updated": "2024-01-01",
    20→                    "language": "Python",
    21→                },
    22→            ],
    23→        },
    24→        {
    25→            "type": "hn",
    26→            "results": [
    27→                {
    28→                    "title": "Ask HN: Best AI finance tools?",
    29→                    "points": 42,
    30→                    "comments": 17,
    31→                    "url": "https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=12345",
    32→                    "date": "2024-03-01",
    33→                },
    34→            ],
    35→        },
    36→    ],
    37→    "signal": {
    38→        "score": 45,
    39→        "verdict": "MODERATE",
    40→        "breakdown": {"github": 30, "hn": 15},
    41→    },
    42→    "meta": {
    43→        "timestamp": "2026-03-07T00:00:00+00:00",
    44→        "version": "0.1.0",
    45→    },
    46→}
    47→
    48→EMPTY_RESULT = {
    49→    "query": "totally obscure niche idea",
    50→    "queries": ["totally obscure niche idea"],
    51→    "sources": [
    52→        {"type": "github", "results": []},
    53→        {"type": "hn", "results": []},
    54→    ],
    55→    "signal": {"score": 0, "verdict": "LOW", "breakdown": {"github": 0, "hn": 0}},
    56→    "meta": {"timestamp": "2026-03-07T00:00:00+00:00", "version": "0.1.0"},
    57→}
    58→
    59→
    60→class TestFormatJson:
    61→    def test_returns_valid_json(self):
    62→        output = format_json(SAMPLE_RESULT)
    63→        parsed = json.loads(output)
    64→        assert parsed["query"] == "AI personal finance tracker"
    65→
    66→    def test_json_has_required_keys(self):
    67→        parsed = json.loads(format_json(SAMPLE_RESULT))
    68→        assert "query" in parsed
    69→        assert "sources" in parsed
    70→        assert "signal" in parsed
    71→        assert "meta" in parsed
    72→
    73→    def test_signal_structure(self):
    74→        parsed = json.loads(format_json(SAMPLE_RESULT))
    75→        signal = parsed["signal"]
    76→        assert "score" in signal
    77→        assert "verdict" in signal
    78→        assert "breakdown" in signal
    79→
    80→    def test_empty_results_valid_json(self):
    81→        output = format_json(EMPTY_RESULT)
    82→        parsed = json.loads(output)
    83→        assert parsed["signal"]["score"] == 0
    84→
    85→
    86→class TestFormatMarkdown:
    87→    def test_contains_header(self):
    88→        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)
    89→        assert "## 🔍 Prior Art Scan" in output
    90→
    91→    def test_contains_signal(self):
    92→        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)
    93→        assert "45/100" in output
    94→
    95→    def test_contains_verdict_moderate(self):
    96→        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)
    97→        assert "MODERATE" in output
    98→
    99→    def test_contains_github_section(self):
   100→        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   101→        assert "### GitHub Repositories" in output
   102→        assert "org/finance-ai" in output
   103→
   104→    def test_contains_hn_section(self):
   105→        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   106→        assert "### Hacker News Discussions" in output
   107→        assert "Ask HN: Best AI finance tools?" in output
   108→
   109→    def test_empty_shows_no_results_message(self):
   110→        output = format_markdown(EMPTY_RESULT)
   111→        assert "No relevant repositories found." in output
   112→        assert "No relevant discussions found." in output
   113→
   114→    def test_pipe_in_description_escaped(self):
   115→        result = {
   116→            **SAMPLE_RESULT,
   117→            "sources": [
   118→                {
   119→                    "type": "github",
   120→                    "results": [
   121→                        {
   122→                            "name": "org/repo",
   123→                            "stars": 100,
   124→                            "description": "A | B tool",
   125→                            "url": "https://github.com/org/repo",
   126→                            "updated": "2024-01-01",
   127→                            "language": "Python",
   128→                        }
   129→                    ],
   130→                },
   131→                {"type": "hn", "results": []},
   132→            ],
   133→        }
   134→        output = format_markdown(result)
   135→        assert "A \\| B tool" in output
   136→
   137→    def test_low_verdict_label(self):
   138→        output = format_markdown(EMPTY_RESULT)
   139→        assert "🟢 LOW" in output
   140→
   141→    def test_high_verdict_label(self):
   142→        high_result = {
   143→            **SAMPLE_RESULT,
   144→            "signal": {
   145→                "score": 75,
   146→                "verdict": "HIGH",
   147→                "breakdown": {"github": 60, "hn": 15},
   148→            },
   149→        }
   150→        output = format_markdown(high_result)
   151→        assert "🔴 HIGH" in output
   152→
   153→
   154→class TestFormatTable:
   155→    def test_contains_title(self):
   156→        output = format_table(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   157→        assert "Prior Art Scan" in output
   158→
   159→    def test_contains_signal(self):
   160→        output = format_table(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   161→        assert "45/100" in output
   162→        assert "MODERATE" in output
   163→
   164→    def test_contains_github_section(self):
   165→        output = format_table(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   166→        assert "GitHub Repositories" in output
   167→        assert "org/finance-ai" in output
   168→
   169→    def test_contains_hn_section(self):
   170→        output = format_table(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   171→        assert "Hacker News Discussions" in output
   172→        assert "Ask HN" in output
   173→
   174→    def test_empty_shows_no_results(self):
   175→        output = format_table(EMPTY_RESULT)
   176→        assert "No relevant repositories found." in output
   177→        assert "No relevant discussions found." in output
   178→
   179→    def test_star_count_comma_formatted(self):
   180→        output = format_table(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   181→        assert "1,234" in output
   182→
   183→    def test_shows_hn_url(self):
   184→        output = format_table(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   185→        assert "https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=12345" in output
   186→
   187→    def test_shows_queries(self):
   188→        output = format_table(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   189→        assert "AI personal finance tracker" in output
   190→
   191→
   192→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   193→# Additional cross-format edge cases
   194→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   195→
   196→
   197→class TestFormatJsonEdgeCases:
   198→    def test_output_is_pretty_printed(self):
   199→        output = format_json(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   200→        # Pretty-printed JSON has newlines and indentation
   201→        assert "
" in output
   202→        assert "  " in output
   203→
   204→    def test_preserves_all_source_types(self):
   205→        parsed = __import__("json").loads(format_json(SAMPLE_RESULT))
   206→        types = [s["type"] for s in parsed["sources"]]
   207→        assert "github" in types
   208→        assert "hn" in types
   209→
   210→
   211→class TestFormatMarkdownEdgeCases:
   212→    def test_shows_queries_in_output(self):
   213→        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   214→        # Queries listed with backtick formatting
   215→        assert "`AI personal finance tracker`" in output
   216→
   217→    def test_caps_repos_at_8_rows(self):
   218→        many_repos = [
   219→            {
   220→                "name": f"org/repo{i}",
   221→                "stars": 100 - i,
   222→                "description": f"Repo {i}",
   223→                "url": f"https://github.com/org/repo{i}",
   224→                "updated": "2024-01-01",
   225→                "language": "Python",
   226→            }
   227→            for i in range(9)
   228→        ]
   229→        result = {
   230→            **SAMPLE_RESULT,
   231→            "sources": [
   232→                {"type": "github", "results": many_repos},
   233→                {"type": "hn", "results": []},
   234→            ],
   235→        }
   236→        output = format_markdown(result)
   237→        # Only first 8 repos should appear; repo8 should NOT
   238→        assert "org/repo7" in output
   239→        assert "org/repo8" not in output
   240→
   241→    def test_hn_shows_points_and_comments(self):
   242→        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   243→        assert "42 pts" in output
   244→        assert "17 comments" in output
   245→
   246→    def test_moderate_verdict_label(self):
   247→        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)
   248→        assert "🟡 MODERATE" in output
   249→

    "content": "     1\u2192\"\"\"Tests for formatters module.\"\"\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192import json\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192from priorart.formatters import format_json, format_markdown, format_table\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192SAMPLE_RESULT = {\n     8\u2192    \"query\": \"AI personal finance tracker\",\n     9\u2192    \"queries\": [\"AI personal finance tracker\", \"AI personal finance\"],\n    10\u2192    \"sources\": [\n    11\u2192        {\n    12\u2192            \"type\": \"github\",\n    13\u2192            \"results\": [\n    14\u2192                {\n    15\u2192                    \"name\": \"org/finance-ai\",\n    16\u2192                    \"stars\": 1234,\n    17\u2192                    \"description\": \"AI-powered finance tracker\",\n    18\u2192                    \"url\": \"https://github.com/org/finance-ai\",\n    19\u2192                    \"updated\": \"2024-01-01\",\n    20\u2192                    \"language\": \"Python\",\n    21\u2192                },\n    22\u2192            ],\n    23\u2192        },\n    24\u2192        {\n    25\u2192            \"type\": \"hn\",\n    26\u2192            \"results\": [\n    27\u2192                {\n    28\u2192                    \"title\": \"Ask HN: Best AI finance tools?\",\n    29\u2192                    \"points\": 42,\n    30\u2192                    \"comments\": 17,\n    31\u2192                    \"url\": \"https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=12345\",\n    32\u2192                    \"date\": \"2024-03-01\",\n    33\u2192                },\n    34\u2192            ],\n    35\u2192        },\n    36\u2192    ],\n    37\u2192    \"signal\": {\n    38\u2192        \"score\": 45,\n    39\u2192        \"verdict\": \"MODERATE\",\n    40\u2192        \"breakdown\": {\"github\": 30, \"hn\": 15},\n    41\u2192    },\n    42\u2192    \"meta\": {\n    43\u2192        \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-07T00:00:00+00:00\",\n    44\u2192        \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n    45\u2192    },\n    46\u2192}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192EMPTY_RESULT = {\n    49\u2192    \"query\": \"totally obscure niche idea\",\n    50\u2192    \"queries\": [\"totally obscure niche idea\"],\n    51\u2192    \"sources\": [\n    52\u2192        {\"type\": \"github\", \"results\": []},\n    53\u2192        {\"type\": \"hn\", \"results\": []},\n    54\u2192    ],\n    55\u2192    \"signal\": {\"score\": 0, \"verdict\": \"LOW\", \"breakdown\": {\"github\": 0, \"hn\": 0}},\n    56\u2192    \"meta\": {\"timestamp\": \"2026-03-07T00:00:00+00:00\", \"version\": \"0.1.0\"},\n    57\u2192}\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192class TestFormatJson:\n    61\u2192    def test_returns_valid_json(self):\n    62\u2192        output = format_json(SAMPLE_RESULT)\n    63\u2192        parsed = json.loads(output)\n    64\u2192        assert parsed[\"query\"] == \"AI personal finance tracker\"\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192    def test_json_has_required_keys(self):\n    67\u2192        parsed = json.loads(format_json(SAMPLE_RESULT))\n    68\u2192        assert \"query\" in parsed\n    69\u2192        assert \"sources\" in parsed\n    70\u2192        assert \"signal\" in parsed\n    71\u2192        assert \"meta\" in parsed\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    def test_signal_structure(self):\n    74\u2192        parsed = json.loads(format_json(SAMPLE_RESULT))\n    75\u2192        signal = parsed[\"signal\"]\n    76\u2192        assert \"score\" in signal\n    77\u2192        assert \"verdict\" in signal\n    78\u2192        assert \"breakdown\" in signal\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192    def test_empty_results_valid_json(self):\n    81\u2192        output = format_json(EMPTY_RESULT)\n    82\u2192        parsed = json.loads(output)\n    83\u2192        assert parsed[\"signal\"][\"score\"] == 0\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192class TestFormatMarkdown:\n    87\u2192    def test_contains_header(self):\n    88\u2192        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)\n    89\u2192        assert \"## \ud83d\udd0d Prior Art Scan\" in output\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192    def test_contains_signal(self):\n    92\u2192        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)\n    93\u2192        assert \"45/100\" in output\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192    def test_contains_verdict_moderate(self):\n    96\u2192        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)\n    97\u2192        assert \"MODERATE\" in output\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192    def test_contains_github_section(self):\n   100\u2192        output = test_contains_hn_section(self):\n   105\u2192        output = format_markdown(SAMPLE_RESULT)\n   106\u2192        assert \"### Hacker News Discussions\" in output\n   107\u2192        assert \"Ask HN: Best AI finance tools?\" in output\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192    def test_empty_shows_no_results_message(self):\n   110\u2192        output = format_markdown(EMPTY_RESULT)\n   111\u2192        assert \"No relevant repositories found.\" in output\n   112\u2192        assert \"No relevant discussions found.\" in output\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    def test_pipe_in_description_escaped(self):\n   115\u2192        result = {\n   116\u2192            **SAMPLE_RESULT,\n   117\u2192            \"sources\": [\n   118\u2192                {\n   119\u2192                    \"type\": \"github\",\n   120\u2192                    \"results\": [\n   121\u2192                        {\n   122\u2192                            \"name\": \"org/repo\",\n   123\u2192                            \"stars\": 100,\n   124\u2192                            \"description\": \"A | B tool\",\n   125\u2192                            \"url\": \"https://github.com/org/repo\",\n   126\u2192 assert \"A \\\\| B tool\" in output\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192    def test_low_verdict_label(self):\n   138\u2192        output = format_markdown(EMPTY_RESULT)\n   139\u2192        assert \"\ud83d\udfe2 LOW\" in output\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192    def test_high_verdict_label(self):\n   142\u2192        high_result = {\n   143\u2192            **SAMPLE_RESULT,\n   144\u2192 159\u2192    def test_contains_signal(self):\n   160\u2192        output = format_table(SAMPLE_RESULT)\n   161\u2192        assert \"45/100\" in output\n   162\u2192        assert \"MODERATE\" in output\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192    def test_contains_github_section(self):\n   165\u2192        output = format_table(SAMPLE_RESULT)\n   166\u2192        assert \"GitHub Repositories\" in output\n   167\u2192        assert \"org/finance-ai\" in output\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192    def test_contains_hn_section(self):\n   170\u2192        output = format_table(SAMPLE_RESULT)\n   171\u2192        assert \"Hacker News Discussions\" in output\n   172\u2192        assert \"Ask HN\" in output\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192    def test_empty_shows_no_results(self):\n   175\u2192        output =
