  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/test_cli.py",
  "content": "\"\"\"CLI integration tests using typer's test client.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nfrom unittest.mock import patch\n\nfrom typer.testing import CliRunner\n\nfrom priorart.cli import app\n\nrunner = CliRunner()\n\nEMPTY_SCAN_RESULT = {\n    \"query\": \"test idea\",\n    \"queries\": [\"test idea\"],\n    \"sources\": [\n        {\"type\": \"github\", \"results\": []},\n        {\"type\": \"hn\", \"results\": []},\n    ],\n    \"signal\": {\"score\": 0, \"verdict\": \"LOW\", \"breakdown\": {\"github\": 0, \"hn\": 0}},\n    \"meta\": {\"timestamp\": \"2026-03-01T00:00:00+00:00\", \"version\": \"0.1.0\"},\n}\n\n\ndef patched_scan(**kwargs):\n    \"\"\"Return a predictable result regardless of inputs.\"\"\"\n    return EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT\n\n\nclass TestCLIHelp:\n    def test_help_exits_zero(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(app, [\"--help\"])\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n\n    def test_help_mentions_scan(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(app, [\"--help\"])\n        assert \"scan\" in result.output\n\n    def test_scan_help_exits_zero(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(app, [\"scan\", \"--help\"])\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n\n    def test_scan_help_mentions_format(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(app, [\"scan\", \"--help\"])\n        assert \"--format\" in result.output\n\n    def test_scan_help_mentions_sources(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(app, [\"scan\", \"--help\"])\n        assert \"--sources\" in result.output\n\n\nclass TestCLIScanFormats:\n    def test_default_format_is_table(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(app, [\"scan\", \"test idea\"])\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n        assert \"Prior Art Scan\" in result.output\n\n    def test_json_format_produces_valid_json(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"fake\"},\n            )\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n        parsed = json.loads(result.output)\n        assert \"query\" in parsed\n        assert \"signal\" in parsed\n        assert \"sources\" in parsed\n        assert \"meta\" in parsed\n\n    def test_markdown_format_has_header(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app, [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"markdown\"]\n            )\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n        assert \"## \ud83d\udd0d Prior Art Scan\" in result.output\n\n    def test_table_format_has_signal(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app, [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"table\"]\n            )\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n        assert \"Reality Signal\" in result.output\n\n    def test_invalid_format_exits_nonzero(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(app, [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"xml\"])\n        assert result.exit_code != 0\n\n    def test_invalid_format_shows_error(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(app, [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"xml\"])\n        assert \"Error\" in result.output or \"invalid\" in result.output.lower()\n\n\nclass TestCLIScanSources:\n    def test_invalid_source_exits_nonzero(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(\n            app, [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--sources\", \"producthunt\"]\n        )\n        assert result.exit_code != 0\n\n    def test_hn_only_source_accepted(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app, [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--sources\", \"hn\", \"--format\", \"json\"]\n            )\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n\n    def test_github_only_warns_without_token(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(\n            app,\n            [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--sources\", \"github\"],\n            env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"\"},\n        )\n        # Should warn but not crash; or produce output\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n\n    def test_github_token_env_var_respected(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT) as mock_scan:\n            runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"ghp_test_token\"},\n            )\n        call_kwargs = mock_scan.call_args[1]\n        assert call_kwargs.get(\"github_token\") == \"ghp_test_token\"\n\n\nclass TestCLIScanOutputStructure:\n    def test_json_output_has_query_field(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"my test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"fake\"},\n            )\n        data = json.loads(result.output)\n        assert data[\"query\"] == \"test idea\"  # from mock\n\n    def test_json_output_signal_has_score(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"fake\"},\n            )\n        data = json.loads(result.output)\n        assert \"score\" in data[\"signal\"]\n        assert isinstance(data[\"signal\"][\"score\"], int)\n\n    def test_json_output_signal_has_verdict(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"fake\"},\n            )\n        data = json.loads(result.output)\n        assert data[\"signal\"][\"verdict\"] in (\"LOW\", \"MODERATE\", \"HIGH\")\n\n    def test_json_output_has_meta_version(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"fake\"},\n            )\n        data = json.loads(result.output)\n        assert \"version\" in data[\"meta\"]\n        assert data[\"meta\"][\"version\"] == \"0.1.0\"\n\n    def test_json_sources_have_type_and_results(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"fake\"},\n            )\n        data = json.loads(result.output)\n        for source in data[\"sources\"]:\n            assert \"type\" in source\n            assert \"results\" in source\n            assert isinstance(source[\"results\"], list)\n\n\nclass TestCLIScanTokenAndSources:\n    def test_github_token_flag_passed_to_scan(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT) as mock_scan:\n            runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\n                    \"scan\",\n                    \"test idea\",\n                    \"--github-token\",\n                    \"ghp_explicit_flag\",\n                    \"--format\",\n                    \"json\",\n                ],\n            )\n        kwargs = mock_scan.call_args[1]\n        assert kwargs.get(\"github_token\") == \"ghp_explicit_flag\"\n\n    def test_both_sources_comma_separated_accepted(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT) as mock_scan:\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--sources\", \"github,hn\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"ghp_test\"},\n            )\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n        sources_arg = (\n            mock_scan.call_args[1].get(\"sources\") or mock_scan.call_args[0][2]\n        )\n        assert \"github\" in sources_arg\n        assert \"hn\" in sources_arg\n\n    def test_github_without_token_warning_on_stderr(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(\n            app,\n            [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--sources\", \"github\"],\n            env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"\"},\n        )\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n        # Note appears in combined output (typer CliRunner merges stderr by default)\n        stderr = result.stderr if hasattr(result, \"stderr\") else \"\"\n        assert (\n            \"Note\" in result.output\n            or \"Note\" in stderr\n            or \"rate-limited\" in result.output\n        )\n\n    def test_sources_passed_to_scan_function(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT) as mock_scan:\n            runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--sources\", \"hn\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n            )\n        kwargs = mock_scan.call_args[1]\n        assert kwargs.get(\"sources\") == [\"hn\"]\n\n    def test_scan_missing_idea_exits_nonzero(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(app, [\"scan\"])\n        assert result.exit_code != 0\n"
}

return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app, [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"markdown\"]\n            )\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n  result = runner.invoke(\n                app, [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"table\"]\n            )\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n        assert \"Reality Signal\" in test_github_only_warns_without_token(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(\n            app,\n            [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--sources\", \"github\"],\n            env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"\"},\n        )\n        # Should warn but not crash; or produce output\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n\n    def test_github_token_env_var_respected(self):\n        with          env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"ghp_test_token\"},\n            )\n        call_kwargs = mock_scan.call_args[1]\n        assert call_kwargs.get(\"github_token\") == \"ghp_test_token\"\n\n\nclass TestCLIScanOutputStructure:\n    def test_json_output_has_query_field(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"fake\"},\n            )\n        data = json.loads(result.output)\n        assert \"score\" in data[\"signal\"]\n        assert isinstance(data[\"signal\"][\"score\"], int)\n\n    def test_json_output_signal_has_verdict(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n test_json_output_has_meta_version(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"fake\"},\n            )\n        data = json.loads(result.output)\n        assert \"version\" in data[\"meta\"]\n        assert data[\"meta\"][\"version\"] == \"0.1.0\"\n\n    def test_json_sources_have_type_and_results(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT):\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"fake\"},\n            )\n        data = json.loads(result.output)\n        for source in data[\"sources\"]:\n            assert \"type\" in source\n            assert \"results\" in source\n            assert isinstance(source[\"results\"], list)\n\n\nclass TestCLIScanTokenAndSources:\n    def test_github_token_flag_passed_to_scan(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT) as mock_scan:\n            runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\n                    \"scan\",\n                    \"test idea\",\n                    \"--github-token\",\n                    \"ghp_explicit_flag\",\n                    \"--format\",\n                    \"json\",\n                ],\n            )\n        kwargs = mock_scan.call_args[1]\n        assert kwargs.get(\"github_token\") == \"ghp_explicit_flag\"\n\n    def test_both_sources_comma_separated_accepted(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT) as mock_scan:\n            result = runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--sources\", \"github,hn\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n                env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"ghp_test\"},\n            )\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n        sources_arg = (\n            mock_scan.call_args[1].get(\"sources\") or mock_scan.call_args[0][2]\n        )\n        assert \"github\" in sources_arg\n        assert \"hn\" in sources_arg\n\n    def test_github_without_token_warning_on_stderr(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(\n            app,\n            [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--sources\", \"github\"],\n            env={\"GITHUB_TOKEN\": \"\"},\n        )\n        assert result.exit_code == 0\n        # Note appears in combined output (typer CliRunner merges stderr by default)\n        stderr = result.stderr if hasattr(result, \"stderr\") else \"\"\n        assert (\n            \"Note\" in result.output\n            or \"Note\" in stderr\n            or \"rate-limited\" in result.output\n        )\n\n    def test_sources_passed_to_scan_function(self):\n        with patch(\"priorart.cli.scan\", return_value=EMPTY_SCAN_RESULT) as mock_scan:\n            runner.invoke(\n                app,\n                [\"scan\", \"test idea\", \"--sources\", \"hn\", \"--format\", \"json\"],\n            )\n        kwargs = mock_scan.call_args[1]\n        assert kwargs.get(\"sources\") == [\"hn\"]\n\n    def test_scan_missing_idea_exits_nonzero(self):\n        result = runner.invoke(app, [\"scan\"])\n        assert result.exit_code != 0\n"

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #133
The import order is wrong. In isort conventions, stdlib imports should come before third-party ones. The order should be: 1. `from __future__ import annotations` 2. stdlib: `from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch` 3. third-party: `import pytest` 4. local: `from priorart.embeddings import ...` But ruff is flagging `from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch` before `import pytest`. According to isort, `from unittest...` is stdlib and `import pytest` is third-party. So the order should be: 1. `from __future__ import annotations` 2. stdlib: `from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch` 3. third-party: `import pytest` 4. local: `from priorart...` With a blank line separating each section. Let me check what the current order is... Actually, looking at the actual imports, they're already in the correct order with proper blank lines between sections. The stdlib imports come before third-party, which comes before local imports. But ruff is flagging this as wrong, so maybe the issue is how isort handles mixed `import` and `from` statements within the same section. Let me reconsider whether `import pytest` should come before `from unittest.mock` since direct imports typically sort before `from` imports in isort's rules.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

"""Tests for the embeddings module."""

from __future__ import annotations

from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

from priorart.embeddings import cosine_similarity, embed_texts, rank_by_similarity

# cosine_similarity

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #136
Interesting - the fix just moved `import pytest` and `from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch` to be separated from the priorart imports. Wait, looking at the output again, it seems it put `from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch` (stdlib) before `import pytest` (third-party), with a blank line between them. That's the correct isort order: `from __future__` → stdlib → third-party → local. Now let me run ruff again to verify everything is clean.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #158
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat: semantic similarity ranking for search results (FRG-255)\n\nAdds OpenAI embedding-based reranking so results that are conceptually\nsimilar to the idea description rank higher even without shared keywords.\n\n- New embeddings.py module: embed_texts (OpenAI API), cosine_similarity,\n  rank_by_similarity\n- scanner.scan() accepts openai_key; when provided, all results are\n  embedded in one API call and reranked by cosine similarity; falls\n  back gracefully on API failure\n- scoring.compute_signal() accepts top_similarity float; adds a\n  semantic score component (0\u201320 pts) to the breakdown and total\n- cli.scan_cmd gains --openai-key / OPENAI_API_KEY option\n- 39 new tests; pre-existing E501 lint issues in test_cli.py fixed\n- Coverage: 98.73% (174 tests passing)\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"",
  "description": "Commit all changes"
}

}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #162
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "gh pr create \\\n  --title \"feat: semantic similarity ranking for search results (FRG-255)\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n\n- Adds OpenAI embedding-based reranking: results conceptually similar to the idea rank higher even without shared keywords (e.g. searching \"document formatter\" surfaces PDF/formatting tools)\n- New `src/priorart/embeddings.py` module with `embed_texts`, `cosine_similarity`, and `rank_by_similarity`\n- `scan()` accepts optional `openai_key`; all results embedded in a single API call, then reranked by cosine similarity; graceful fallback if the API call fails\n- `compute_signal()` accepts optional `top_similarity` float and adds a **semantic score** component (0\u201320 pts) to the breakdown\n- CLI gains `--openai-key` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var support\n- 39 new tests; pre-existing E501 lint issues in `test_cli.py` cleaned up\n\n## Behaviour\n\nWithout `--openai-key`: existing behaviour unchanged (results sorted by stars/points).\n\nWith `--openai-key`: results get a `similarity` field and are sorted by it; `signal.breakdown` gains a `\"semantic\"` key; score receives a boost of up to 20 pts.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] `ruff check src tests` \u2014 clean\n- [x] `pytest` \u2014 174 tests passing\n- [x] Coverage 98.73% (\u2265 60% CI threshold)\n- [ ] Manual smoke test with real OpenAI key and a query like \"document formatter\"\n\nCloses FRG-255\n\n\ud83e\udd16 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\"",
  "description": "Create pull request"
}

    "input": {
      "command": "gh pr create \\\n  --title \"feat: semantic similarity ranking for search results (FRG-255)\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n\n- Adds OpenAI embedding-based reranking: results conceptually similar to the idea rank higher even without shared keywords (e.g. searching \"document formatter\" surfaces PDF/formatting tools)\n- New `src/priorart/embeddings.py` module with `embed_texts`, `cosine_similarity`, and `rank_by_similarity`\n- `scan()` accepts optional `openai_key`; all results embedded in a single API call, then reranked by cosine similarity; graceful fallback if the API call fails\n- `compute_signal()` accepts optional `top_similarity` float and adds a **semantic score** component (0\u201320 pts) to the breakdown\n- CLI gains `--openai-key` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var support\n- 39 new tests; pre-existing E501 lint issues in `test_cli.py` cleaned up\n\n## Behaviour\n\nWithout `--openai-key`: existing behaviour unchanged (results sorted by stars/points).\n\nWith `--openai-key`: results get a `similarity` field and are sorted by it; `signal.breakdown` gains a `\"semantic\"` key; score receives a boost of up to 20 pts.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] `ruff check src tests` \u2014 clean\n- [x] `pytest` \u2014 174 tests passing\n- [x] Coverage 98.73% (\u2265 60% CI threshold)\n- [ ] Manual smoke test with real OpenAI key and a query like \"document formatter\"\n\nCloses FRG-255\n\n\ud83e\udd16 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\"",
      "description": "Create pull request"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
{"id":"657d19ed-babb-452d-9dec-55b6255b500d","identifier":"FRG-255","title":"Semantic similarity ranking for search results","description":"## What

Rank results by semantic similarity to the idea description, not just keyword overlap.

## Scope

* Embed the idea description and each result's description/title
* Rank results by cosine similarity
* Use an embedding API (OpenAI embeddings or similar)
* Results that are conceptually similar rank higher even without shared keywords

## Acceptance Criteria

* Results are reordered by semantic relevance
* A search for \"document formatter\" surfaces PDF/formatting tools even if they don't contain those exact words
* Signal score incorporates semantic similarity","projectMilestone":{"id":"690da7a4-d5de-4069-aa14-00aab172ab85","name":"M2: Semantic Search"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-255/semantic-similarity-ranking-for-search-results","gitBranchName":"devlab/frg-255-semantic-similarity-ranking-for-search-results","createdAt":"2026-03-09T05:04:07.820Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-09T10:44:54.609Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Test in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"ade651d0-dbeb-4f7f-ad94-50c19f645b70","title":"feat: semantic similarity ranking for search results (FRG-255)","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/priorart/pull/2"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Project Manager","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Project Manager","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Prior Art Scanner","projectId":"4c456b8e-532a-45d5-9bea-67dde700b251","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #172
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/.claude/projects/-home-gem-project/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "content": "# Project Memory: priorart\n\n## Project Overview\nPrior art scanner CLI (`priorart`). Searches GitHub repos and HN discussions to assess idea competition, produces a Reality Signal score (0\u2013100).\n\n## Package Structure\n```\nsrc/priorart/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 __init__.py       # version\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 scanner.py        # orchestrator + query builder\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 scoring.py        # reality signal computation\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 formatters.py     # JSON, markdown, table output\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cli.py            # typer CLI entry point\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 embeddings.py     # OpenAI embedding + cosine similarity (added FRG-255)\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 sources/\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 github.py     # GitHub repository search\n    \u2514\u2500\u2500 hn.py         # Hacker News search (Algolia API)\n```\n\n## Dev Workflow\n- Install: `uv sync --extra dev`\n- Lint: `uv run ruff check src tests` (uses ruff; select = [\"E\", \"F\", \"I\"], max line = 88)\n- Tests: `uv run pytest --tb=short -q`\n- Coverage: `uv run pytest --cov=src/priorart --cov-fail-under=60`\n\n## Key Conventions\n- Python 3.11+, `from __future__ import annotations` in all modules\n- httpx for HTTP calls (no requests)\n- typer for CLI\n- All tests use `unittest.mock.patch`, no external API calls in tests\n- Test class names: `TestFooBar`, methods: `test_verb_noun`\n- Signal score: GitHub (0-60) + HN (0-40) + Semantic (0-20 when openai_key provided), capped at 100\n\n## Notes\n- `uv run` is the way to run things; bare `ruff`/`pytest` not on PATH\n- Pre-existing E501 lint errors in test_cli.py were fixed in FRG-255\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01N33NAa8K8KxZLKH5yAi2fw",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/.claude/projects/-home-gem-project/memory/MEMORY.md",
      "content": "# Project Memory: priorart\n\n## Project Overview\nPrior art scanner CLI (`priorart`). Searches GitHub repos and HN discussions to assess idea competition, produces a Reality Signal score (0\u2013100).\n\n## Package Structure\n```\nsrc/priorart/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 __init__.py       # version\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 scanner.py        # orchestrator + query builder\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 scoring.py        # reality signal computation\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 formatters.py     # JSON, markdown, table output\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cli.py            # typer CLI entry point\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 embeddings.py     # OpenAI embedding + cosine similarity (added FRG-255)\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 sources/\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 github.py     # GitHub repository search\n    \u2514\u2500\u2500 hn.py         # Hacker News search (Algolia API)\n```\n\n## Dev Workflow\n- Install: `uv sync --extra dev`\n- Lint: `uv run ruff check src tests` (uses ruff; select = [\"E\", \"F\", \"I\"], max line = 88)\n- Tests: `uv run pytest --tb=short -q`\n- Coverage: `uv run pytest --cov=src/priorart --cov-fail-under=60`\n\n## Key Conventions\n- Python 3.11+, `from __future__ import annotations` in all modules\n- httpx for HTTP calls (no requests)\n- typer for CLI\n- All tests use `unittest.mock.patch`, no external API calls in tests\n- Test class names: `TestFooBar`, methods: `test_verb_noun`\n- Signal score: GitHub (0-60) + HN (0-40) + Semantic (0-20 when openai_key provided), capped at 100\n\n## Notes\n- `uv run` is the way to run things; bare `ruff`/`pytest` not on PATH\n- Pre-existing E501 lint errors in test_cli.py were fixed in FRG-255\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #174
## Status DONE ## What Changed - `src/priorart/embeddings.py` — new module: `embed_texts` (OpenAI API via httpx), `cosine_similarity`, `rank_by_similarity` - `src/priorart/scoring.py` — `compute_signal()` gains optional `top_similarity` param; adds semantic score component (0–20 pts) to breakdown - `src/priorart/scanner.py` — `scan()` gains `openai_key` param; embeds idea + all results in one API call, reranks by cosine similarity, computes `top_similarity` for signal; graceful fallback on API errors - `src/priorart/cli.py` — `scan_cmd` gains `--openai-key` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var option - `tests/test_embeddings.py` — new: 20 tests covering cosine_similarity, embed_texts, rank_by_similarity - `tests/test_scoring.py` — 11 new tests for the semantic score component - `tests/test_scanner.py` — 9 new tests for semantic reranking in `scan()` - `tests/test_cli.py` — pre-existing E501 line-length issues fixed (no behaviour change) - `uv.lock` — updated with new dev deps (ruff, pytest-cov, etc.) ## Key Decisions - Used `httpx` directly for OpenAI API calls (already a dependency) rather than adding `openai` SDK — keeps the dependency footprint minimal - Semantic reranking is fully optional (no `openai_key` → identical behaviour to v0.1.1) - Single batched embedding call for all texts (idea + all repos + all HN) to minimise API latency and cost - `top_similarity` = average of top-3 results across both sources, giving a stable signal even with few results ## PR / Branch - Branch: `feature/frg-255-semantic-similarity-ranking` - PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/priorart/pull/2
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "## Status\nDONE\n\n## What Changed\n- `src/priorart/embeddings.py` \u2014 new module: `embed_texts` (OpenAI API via httpx), `cosine_similarity`, `rank_by_similarity`\n- `src/priorart/scoring.py` \u2014 `compute_signal()` gains optional `top_similarity` param; adds semantic score component (0\u201320 pts) to breakdown\n- `src/priorart/scanner.py` \u2014 `scan()` gains `openai_key` param; embeds idea + all results in one API call, reranks by cosine similarity, computes `top_similarity` for signal; graceful fallback on API errors\n- `src/priorart/cli.py` \u2014 `scan_cmd` gains `--openai-key` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var option\n- `tests/test_embeddings.py` \u2014 new: 20 tests covering cosine_similarity, embed_texts, rank_by_similarity\n- `tests/test_scoring.py` \u2014 11 new tests for the semantic score component\n- `tests/test_scanner.py` \u2014 9 new tests for semantic reranking in `scan()`\n- `tests/test_cli.py` \u2014 pre-existing E501 line-length issues fixed (no behaviour change)\n- `uv.lock` \u2014 updated with new dev deps (ruff, pytest-cov, etc.)\n\n## Key Decisions\n- Used `httpx` directly for OpenAI API calls (already a dependency) rather than adding `openai` SDK \u2014 keeps the dependency footprint minimal\n- Semantic reranking is fully optional (no `openai_key` \u2192 identical behaviour to v0.1.1)\n- Single batched embedding call for all texts (idea + all repos + all HN) to minimise API latency and cost\n- `top_similarity` = average of top-3 results across both sources, giving a stable signal even with few results\n\n## PR / Branch\n- Branch: `feature/frg-255-semantic-similarity-ranking`\n- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/priorart/pull/2"
  }
}
